name: Example SL-2 (live preset + pattern over BLE)
_comment: Showcase: drive a Boss SL-2 over BLE-MIDI with no laptop. The SL-2 has NO Program Change and the manual calls slicer Type/pattern front-panel-only, but it parses Roland DT1 SysEx on its TRS MIDI IN, so we recall a stored preset and override pattern/type/tempo live. DT1 is device-id based (0x10) and channel-independent; only the channel-voice CCs (on/off, EXP) use the SL-2 receive channel. See data/scenes/README.md for the framing + checksum recipe.

events.comment: PATCH_SELECT: recall stored pattern 7 into the temp/live buffer (0x7F000100 = 00 07). This is live preset recall over MIDI, which the SL-2 manual says is impossible.
SLICER(1) PATTERN = 10 (temp patch 0x20001000, range 0-50). Overrides the recalled preset's pattern live.
SYSTEM TEMPO = 120.0 BPM (0x10000000, INTEGER4x4 = BPM*10 = 1200 = 0x04B0 nibble-split to 00 04 0B 00). The 19-byte frame is why we raise the BLE MTU.
EFFECTS ON (CC#80 = 127). Channel-voice: set channel to the SL-2's receive channel (example uses 1).
